<content>1: import { join } from "path";
2: import { existsSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, readdirSync, rmdirSync, unlinkSync } from "fs";
3: import { getPaths } from "../config/paths.js";
4: import { dump, load } from "js-yaml";
5: import crypto from "crypto";
6: 
7: export type AgentStatus = 
8:   | "online" 
9:   | "offline";
10: 
11: export interface Agent {
12:   id: string;
13:   owner: string;
14:   token: string;
15:   sessionIds: string[];
16:   status: AgentStatus;
17:   startedAt: Date;
18:   updatedAt: Date;
19:   lastActivityAt?: Date;
20: }
21: 
22: export interface AgentData {
23:   id: string;
24:   owner: string;
25:   token: string;
26:   sessionIds: string[];
27:   status: AgentStatus;
28:   startedAt: string;
29:   updatedAt: string;
30:   lastActivityAt?: string;
31: }
32: 
33: export interface CreateAgentInput {
34:   owner: string;
35: }
36: 
37: export class AgentRepository {
38:   private getAgentPath(agentId: string): string {
39:     return join(getPaths().agents, agentId);
40:   }
41: 
42:   private getAgentFilePath(agentId: string): string {
43:     return join(this.getAgentPath(agentId), "agent.yaml");
44:   }
45: 
46:   private generateId(): string {
47:     return crypto.randomUUID();
48:   }
49: 
50:   async create(input: CreateAgentInput): Promise<Agent> {
51:     const id = this.generateId();
52:     const agentPath = this.getAgentPath(id);
53:     
54:     if (!existsSync(agentPath)) {
55:       mkdirSync(agentPath, { recursive: true });
56:     }
57: 
58:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
59:     const agentData: AgentData = {
60:       id,
61:       owner: input.owner,
62:       token: crypto.randomUUID(), // Temporary placeholder, service will update with JWT
63:       sessionIds: [],
64:       status: "offline",
65:       startedAt: now,
66:       updatedAt: now,
67:     };
68: 
69:     writeFileSync(this.getAgentFilePath(id), dump(agentData), "utf-8");
70: 
71:     return {
72:       ...agentData,
73:       startedAt: new Date(agentData.startedAt),
74:       updatedAt: new Date(agentData.updatedAt),
75:       lastActivityAt: agentData.lastActivityAt 
76:         ? new Date(agentData.lastActivityAt) 
77:         : undefined,
78:     };
79:   }
80: 
81:   async findById(agentId: string): Promise<Agent | null> {
82:     const filePath = this.getAgentFilePath(agentId);
83:     if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
84:       return null;
85:     }
86: 
87:     const content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
88:     const data = load(content) as AgentData;
89: 
90:     return {
91:       ...data,
92:       startedAt: new Date(data.startedAt),
93:       updatedAt: new Date(data.updatedAt),
94:       lastActivityAt: data.lastActivityAt 
95:         ? new Date(data.lastActivityAt) 
96:         : undefined,
97:     };
98:   }
99: 
100:   async findByStatus(status: AgentStatus): Promise<Agent[]> {
101:     const Paths = getPaths();
102:     if (!existsSync(Paths.agents)) {
103:       return [];
104:     }
105: 
106:     const entries = readdirSync(Paths.agents, { withFileTypes: true });
107:     const agents: Agent[] = [];
108: 
109:     for (const entry of entries) {
110:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
111:         const agentFile = join(Paths.agents, entry.name, "agent.yaml");
112:         if (existsSync(agentFile)) {
113:           const content = readFileSync(agentFile, "utf-8");
114:           const data = load(content) as AgentData;
115:           if (data.status === status) {
116:             agents.push({
117:               ...data,
118:               startedAt: new Date(data.startedAt),
119:               updatedAt: new Date(data.updatedAt),
120:               lastActivityAt: data.lastActivityAt 
121:                 ? new Date(data.lastActivityAt) 
122:                 : undefined,
123:             });
124:           }
125:         }
126:       }
127:     }
128: 
129:     return agents.sort((a, b) => b.startedAt.getTime() - a.startedAt.getTime());
130:   }
131: 
132:   async findByOwner(owner: string): Promise<Agent[]> {
133:     const Paths = getPaths();
134:     if (!existsSync(Paths.agents)) {
135:       return [];
136:     }
137: 
138:     const entries = readdirSync(Paths.agents, { withFileTypes: true });
139:     const agents: Agent[] = [];
140: 
141:     for (const entry of entries) {
142:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
143:         const agentFile = join(Paths.agents, entry.name, "agent.yaml");
144:         if (existsSync(agentFile)) {
145:           const content = readFileSync(agentFile, "utf-8");
146:           const data = load(content) as AgentData;
147:           if (data.owner === owner) {
148:             agents.push({
149:               ...data,
150:               startedAt: new Date(data.startedAt),
151:               updatedAt: new Date(data.updatedAt),
152:               lastActivityAt: data.lastActivityAt 
153:                 ? new Date(data.lastActivityAt) 
154:                 : undefined,
155:             });
156:           }
157:         }
158:       }
159:     }
160: 
161:     return agents.sort((a, b) => b.startedAt.getTime() - a.startedAt.getTime());
162:   }
163: 
164:   async update(
165:     agentId: string, 
166:     updates: Partial<Omit<AgentData, "id" | "startedAt">>
167:   ): Promise<Agent | null> {
168:     const agent = await this.findById(agentId);
169:     if (!agent) return null;
170: 
171:     const updatedData: AgentData = {
172:       ...agent,
173:       ...updates,
174:       updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
175:     };
176: 
177:     const filePath = this.getAgentFilePath(agentId);
178:     writeFileSync(filePath, dump(updatedData), "utf-8");
179: 
180:     return {
181:       ...updatedData,
182:       startedAt: new Date(updatedData.startedAt),
183:       updatedAt: new Date(updatedData.updatedAt),
184:       lastActivityAt: updatedData.lastActivityAt 
185:         ? new Date(updatedData.lastActivityAt) 
186:         : undefined,
187:     };
188:   }
189: 
190:   async updateStatus(agentId: string, status: AgentStatus): Promise<Agent | null> {
191:     return this.update(agentId, { status });
192:   }
193: 
194:   async updateLastActivity(agentId: string): Promise<Agent | null> {
195:     return this.update(agentId, { lastActivityAt: new Date().toISOString() });
196:   }
197: 
198:   async assignSession(agentId: string, sessionId: string): Promise<Agent | null> {
199:     const agent = await this.findById(agentId);
200:     if (!agent) return null;
201:     
202:     if (!agent.sessionIds.includes(sessionId)) {
203:       const updatedSessionIds = [...agent.sessionIds, sessionId];
204:       return this.update(agentId, { sessionIds: updatedSessionIds });
205:     }
206:     return agent;
207:   }
208: 
209:   async unassignSession(agentId: string, sessionId: string): Promise<Agent | null> {
210:     const agent = await this.findById(agentId);
211:     if (!agent) return null;
212:     
213:     const updatedSessionIds = agent.sessionIds.filter(id => id !== sessionId);
214:     return this.update(agentId, { sessionIds: updatedSessionIds });
215:   }
216: 
217:   async setSessionAssignment(agentId: string, sessionId: string, sessionRepo: any): Promise<boolean> {
218:     // Update agent's sessionIds
219:     const agent = await this.assignSession(agentId, sessionId);
220:     if (!agent) return false;
221:     
222:     // Update session's assignedAgentId
223:     await sessionRepo.update(sessionId, { assignedAgentId: agentId });
224:     return true;
225:   }
226: 
227:   async unsetSessionAssignment(agentId: string, sessionId: string, sessionRepo: any): Promise<boolean> {
228:     // Update agent's sessionIds
229:     const agent = await this.unassignSession(agentId, sessionId);
230:     if (!agent) return false;
231:     
232:     // Update session's assignedAgentId
233:     await sessionRepo.update(sessionId, { assignedAgentId: null });
234:     return true;
235:   }
236: 
237:   async delete(agentId: string): Promise<void> {
238:     const agentPath = this.getAgentPath(agentId);
239:     if (existsSync(agentPath)) {
240:       this.deleteDirectoryRecursive(agentPath);
241:     }
242:   }
243: 
244:   private deleteDirectoryRecursive(dirPath: string): void {
245:     if (!existsSync(dirPath)) return;
246: 
247:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
248:     
249:     for (const entry of entries) {
250:       const entryPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
251:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
252:         this.deleteDirectoryRecursive(entryPath);
253:       } else {
254:         unlinkSync(entryPath);
255:       }
256:     }
257: 
258:     rmdirSync(dirPath);
259:   }
260: 
261:   async exists(agentId: string): Promise<boolean> {
262:     return existsSync(this.getAgentFilePath(agentId));
263:   }
264: }
265: 
266: export const agentRepository = new AgentRepository();

56:     return join(this.getSessionPath(projectId, sessionId), "checkout");
57:   }
58: 
59:   private generateId(): string {
60:     return crypto.randomUUID();
61:   }
62: 
63:   async create(input: CreateSessionInput): Promise<Session> {
64:     const id = this.generateId();
65:     const sessionPath = this.getSessionPath(input.projectId, id);
66:     const upstreamPath = this.getUpstreamPath(input.projectId, id);
67:     const checkoutPath = this.getCheckoutPath(input.projectId, id);
68:     
69:     // Create session directory
70:     if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
71:       mkdirSync(sessionPath, { recursive: true });
72:     }
73: 
74:     // Create upstream directory
75:     if (!existsSync(upstreamPath)) {
76:       mkdirSync(upstreamPath, { recursive: true });
77:     }
78: 
79:     // Create checkout directory
80:     if (!existsSync(checkoutPath)) {
81:       mkdirSync(checkoutPath, { recursive: true });
82:     }
83: 
84:     const now = new Date().toISOString();
85:     const sessionData: SessionData = {
86:       id,
87:       name: input.name,
88:       projectId: input.projectId,
89:       owner: input.owner,
90:       upstreamPath,
91:       checkoutPath,
92:       assignedAgentId: input.assignedAgentId,
93:       status: "active",
94:       createdAt: now,
95:       updatedAt: now,
96:     };
97: 
98:     writeFileSync(
99:       this.getSessionFilePath(input.projectId, id),
100:       dump(sessionData),
101:       "utf-8"
102:     );
103: 
104:     return {
105:       ...sessionData,
106:       createdAt: new Date(sessionData.createdAt),
107:       updatedAt: new Date(sessionData.updatedAt),
108:     };
109:   }
110: 
111:   async findById(sessionId: string): Promise<Session | null> {
112:     // Search across all projects for the session
113:     const Paths = getPaths();
114:     if (!existsSync(Paths.projects)) {
115:       return null;
116:     }
117: 
118:     const projectEntries = readdirSync(Paths.projects, { withFileTypes: true });
119:     
120:     for (const projectEntry of projectEntries) {
121:       if (projectEntry.isDirectory()) {
122:         const sessionsDir = join(Paths.projects, projectEntry.name, "sessions");
123:         if (existsSync(sessionsDir)) {
124:           const sessionFile = join(sessionsDir, sessionId, "session.yaml");
125:           if (existsSync(sessionFile)) {
126:             const content = readFileSync(sessionFile, "utf-8");
127:             const data = load(content) as SessionData;
128:             return {
129:               ...data,
130:               createdAt: new Date(data.createdAt),
131:               updatedAt: new Date(data.updatedAt),
132:             };
133:           }
134:         }
135:       }
136:     }
137: 
138:     return null;
139:   }
140: 
141:   async findByProjectAndId(projectId: string, sessionId: string): Promise<Session | null> {

143:     if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
144:       return null;

157:   async listByProject(projectId: string): Promise<Session[]> {
158:     const sessionsDir = join(getPaths().projects, projectId, "sessions");
159:     if (!existsSync(sessionsDir)) {
160:       return [];
161:     }
162: 
163:     const entries = readdirSync(sessionsDir, { withFileTypes: true });
164:     const sessions: Session[] = [];
165: 
166:     for (const entry of entries) {

169:         if (existsSync(sessionFile)) {
170:           const content = readFileSync(sessionFile, "utf-8");
171:           const data = load(content) as SessionData;
172:           sessions.push({
173:             ...data,
174:             createdAt: new Date(data.createdAt),
175:             updatedAt: new Date(data.updatedAt),
176:           });
177:         }
178:       }
179:     }
180: 
181:     return sessions.sort((a, b) => b.createdAt.getTime() - a.createdAt.getTime());
182:   }

185:     const Paths = getPaths();
186:     if (!existsSync(Paths.projects)) {
187:       return [];
188:     }
189: 
190:     const projectEntries = readdirSync(Paths.projects, { withFileTypes: true });

199:             if (entry.isDirectory()) {
200:               const sessionFile = join(sessionsDir, entry.name, "session.yaml");
201:               if (existsSync(sessionFile)) {
202:                 const content = readFileSync(sessionFile, "utf-8");
203:                 const data = load(content) as SessionData;

205:                   sessions.push({

209:                   });

212:             }
213:           }
214:         }

216:     }
217: 
218:     return sessions.sort((a, b) => b.createdAt.getTime() - a.createdAt.getTime());

220: 
221:   async update(sessionId: string, updates: Partial<Omit<SessionData, "id" | "createdAt">>): Promise<Session | null> {
222:     const session = await this.findById(sessionId);
223:     if (!session) return null;
224:

226:       ...session,

240: 
241:   async delete(projectId: string, sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
242:     const sessionPath = this.getSessionPath(projectId, sessionId);
243:     
244:     // Delete entire session directory (includes upstream/, checkout/, repo.fossil, session.yaml)
245:     if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
246:       this.deleteDirectoryRecursive(sessionPath);
247:     }
248:   }
249: 
250:   private deleteDirectoryRecursive(dirPath: string): void {
251:     if (!existsSync(dirPath)) return;
252: 
253:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
254:     
255:     for (const entry of entries) {
256:       const entryPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
257:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
258:         this.deleteDirectoryRecursive(entryPath);
259:       } else {
260:         unlinkSync(entryPath);
261:       }
262:     }
263: 
264:     rmdirSync(dirPath);
265:   }
266: 
267:   async exists(projectId: string, sessionId: string): Promise<boolean> {
268:     return existsSync(this.getSessionFilePath(projectId, sessionId));
269:   }
270: }

4. For each active session, it starts a fossil server and sends `session_ready`

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 01:33:52)

Let me trace through the complete flow now.

Looking at the architecture:

1. **Session Creation** (sessions/routes.tsx:102-174):
   - Creates session directories (upstream/, checkout/)
   - Clones repo to upstream/
   - Imports to fossil (`repo.fossil`)
   - Opens fossil checkout
   - But does NOT start the fossil server or assign an agent

2. **Agent Creation** (agents/routes.tsx:233-240):
   - Creates agent with token
   - Agent is offline initially
   - No session assignment

3. **Agent Connection** (index.tsx:192-241):
   - Agent connects via WebSocket
   - If agent has `sessionIds` (assigned sessions), platform starts Fossil servers
   - Sends `session_ready` message with sessions and ports

4. **The Problem**:
   - The `mimo-agent` doesn't handle `session_ready` message
   - It doesn't know which directory to work in
   - It doesn't know the fossil server URL

Wait, let me check if there's a UI for assigning agents to sessions...

Looking at SessionCreatePage:
- It takes an `assignedAgentId` as optional input
- So sessions CAN be created with an assigned agent

But if the session is created with an agent, what happens?
- Session is created
- But the agent isn't notified - it needs to be running and connected!

So the timing issue is:
1. Agent needs to be running and connected FIRST
2. THEN session can be created with that agent assigned
3. OR session is created first, then agent is assigned later

Looking at the code again:
- When agent connects, it looks at `agent.sessionIds` and starts Fossil servers
- But `session_ready` message is sent but not handled by agent

Also, the agent has a `workDir` parameter - but it's passed as `--workdir` at startup. If an agent handles multiple sessions... that's not going to work.
 
The agent's current work directory is fixed at startup through a command-line argument. When `session_ready` is transmitted, it carries critical session details like ID and port on the Fossil server. However, the agent lacks a mechanism to process this message or dynamically change its working context.

## Discovery Summary

**The fossil proxy server is not executing in mimo-platform** appears to be true in a sense - the `FossilServerManager` exists and works (tests pass), but there's a **mismatch in how it's integrated with mimo-agent**:

### Key Findings:

1. **Platform sends `session_ready` message** (`index.tsx:235-239`) with `{sessionId, port}` to the agent

2. **Agent doesn't handle `session_ready`** - the message falls through to `default` case and logs "Unknown message type"

3. **Agent uses a single `workDir`** passed at startup - it doesn't dynamically change working directories per session

4. **Timing dependency**: The fossil server only starts when:
   - Agent connects (WebSocket open)
   - Agent has sessions assigned (`agent.sessionIds`)
   - Sessions have `status === "active"`

5. **Fossil path computation issue**:
   - In `index.tsx:219`: `const fossilPath = \`${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil\``
   - In `sessions/routes.tsx:145`: `const fossilPath = \`${session.upstreamPath}/../repo.fossil\``
   - These should be equivalent, but the session needs the fossil repo created first

##### Design Questions Raised:

| Question | Current State | Implication |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Can one agent handle multiple sessions? | Agent has `sessionIds: string[]` | But only single `workDir` - potential conflict |
| When should fossil server start? | Agent connects OR session created | If session first, no server until agent connects |
| How does agent know checkout path? | Not transmitted | Agent watches wrong directory or errors |
| What does agent do with `session_ready`? | Ignores it | No dynamic session handling |

Would you like me to dig deeper into any specific aspect of this gap? For example:
- The intended design per the OpenSpec artifacts
- How the agent-to-session mapping should work
- When fossil servers should be started (session creation vs agent connection)

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Proposal: mimo-platform
2: 
3: ## Why
4: 
5: Emacs is outdated for modern collaborative development. We need a web-based editor that combines the power of worktree-based development with AI agents, allowing users to chat with agents that code while maintaining full version control. Unlike traditional IDEs, this should be minimal, text-focused, and filesystem-driven—no databases, no bloat, just Emacs-style efficiency in the browser.
6: 
7: ## What Changes
8: 
9: - **New System**: Build mimo-platform using TypeScript/Bun with Elysia framework for minimal footprint
10: - **Auth System**: Simple username/password login/logout without email confirmation, stored in YAML files
11: - **Project Management**: Create projects, link to Git/Fossil repositories, switch between projects
12: - **Session Management**: Create sessions with titles that spawn worktrees, chat interface with streaming responses, file tree with change indicators, syntax-highlighted diffs
13: - **Agent Management**: Create agents that generate tokens, spawn mimo-agent processes that proxy ACP protocol, list active agents
14: - **VCS Integration**: Support both Git (native worktrees) and Fossil (virtual worktree via multiple clones), commit and push to remote
15: - **mimo-agent**: Build separate TypeScript/Bun single-file executable that connects via WebSocket, clones from Fossil server, proxies ACP, watches files, and reports changes
16: - **UI Style**: Emacs-inspired minimal interface with buffers, keybindings, split windows
17: 
18: ## Capabilities
19: 
20: ### New Capabilities
21: - `user-auth`: Simple username/password authentication, filesystem-based credential storage
22: - `project-management`: CRUD operations for projects, repository linking, project switching
23: - `session-management`: Session CRUD with worktree creation, chat with streaming, file viewing with syntax highlighting
24: - `agent-lifecycle`: Agent creation with token generation, process spawning, connection management
25: - `vcs-integration`: Git and Fossil repository support, worktree management, commit/push operations
26: - `file-sync`: Bidirectional file synchronization between agent worktree and original repository
27: 
28: ### Modified Capabilities
29: - None
30: 
31: ## Impact
32: 
33: - **New Dependencies**: Elixir/Phoenix, Hologram, Bun (for agent build), Fossil
34: - **Filesystem Structure**: Creates `~/.mimo/` directory structure for projects, sessions, and chat history
35: - **Network**: WebSocket connections between browser-platform-agent
36: - **Processes**: Fossil server per session, mimo-agent process per session
37: - **Distribution**: mimo-agent as separate downloadable binary

(End of file - total 37 lines)
</content>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: Agent reports file changes to platform
4: The system SHALL receive file change notifications from mimo-agent.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Single file change
7: - **WHEN** agent modifies file "src/app.js"
8: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends WebSocket message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/app.js"]}
9: - **AND** platform marks file with [M] indicator
10: 
11: #### Scenario: Multiple file changes
12: - **WHEN** agent modifies multiple files
13: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends single message with all changed files
14: - **AND** platform updates all file indicators
15: 
16: #### Scenario: New file created
17: - **WHEN** agent creates new file "src/new.ts"
18: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/new.ts"], is_new: true}
19: - **AND** platform marks file with [?] indicator
20: 
21: ### Requirement: Platform synchronizes changes to original repository
22: The system SHALL copy changed files from agent worktree to original repository worktree.
23: 
24: #### Scenario: Sync modified file
25: - **WHEN** platform receives file change notification
26: - **THEN** system copies file from session worktree to original repo worktree
27: - **AND** system preserves file permissions
28: 
29: #### Scenario: Sync new file
30: - **WHEN** platform receives new file notification
31: - **THEN** system copies file and creates parent directories if needed
32: - **AND** system preserves file permissions
33: 
34: #### Scenario: Batch sync on reconnect
35: - **WHEN** user reconnects after offline period
36: - **THEN** system receives all buffered file changes
37: - **AND** system syncs all files to original repo worktree
38: 
39: ### Requirement: Platform detects conflicts
40: The system SHALL identify when agent changes conflict with original repository changes.
41: 
42: #### Scenario: Conflict detection
43: - **WHEN** platform attempts sync and detects file was modified in original repo
44: - **THEN** system marks file with conflict indicator [!]
45: - **AND** system displays "Conflicts detected" in changes buffer
46: - **AND** system requires manual resolution
47: 
48: #### Scenario: Show conflict details
49: - **WHEN** user clicks conflicted file
50: - **THEN** system displays diff between agent version and original repo version
51: 
52: ### Requirement: Platform handles file deletions
53: The system SHALL detect and sync file deletions.
54: 
55: #### Scenario: File deleted by agent
56: - **WHEN** agent deletes file "src/old.js"
57: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/old.js"], deleted: true}
58: - **AND** platform marks file with [D] indicator
59: - **AND** system removes file from original repo worktree
60: 
61: ### Requirement: File sync is bidirectional
62: The system SHALL support manual sync from original repo to session.
63: 
64: #### Scenario: Pull latest changes
65: - **WHEN** user requests sync from original repo
66: - **THEN** system copies changes from original repo to session worktree
67: - **AND** system updates file tree indicators
68: - **AND** system notifies agent of changes
69: 
70: #### Scenario: Sync conflicts with agent changes
71: - **WHEN** pull conflicts with uncommitted agent changes
72: - **THEN** system displays conflict warning
73: - **AND** system requires manual resolution

(End of file - total 73 lines)

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/session-management/spec.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: User can create a session
4: The system SHALL allow users to create sessions within a project. Each session creates a worktree and starts a Fossil server.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Create session with title
7: - **WHEN** authenticated user submits session title "fix-auth-bug" for project "my-app"
8: - **THEN** system creates directory ~/.mimo/projects/my-app/sessions/fix-auth-bug/
9: - **AND** system clones project's repository to repo.fossil
10: - **AND** system assigns auto-generated port (8000-9000 range)
11: - **AND** system starts Fossil server on assigned port
12: - **AND** system stores session.yaml with {title: "fix-auth-bug", port: <assigned>, status: "active"}
13: - **AND** system displays session view
14: 
15: #### Scenario: Port collision handling
16: - **WHEN** system attempts to start Fossil server on occupied port
17: - **THEN** system tries next available port in range
18: - **AND** system continues until successful
19: 
20: #### Scenario: Duplicate session title
21: - **WHEN** user submits session title that already exists in project
22: - **THEN** system appends timestamp to title or returns error
23: 
24: ### Requirement: Session persists across disconnects
25: The system SHALL maintain session state when user disconnects.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: Reconnect to active session
28: - **WHEN** user reconnects to existing session
29: - **THEN** system loads chat history from messages.jsonl
30: - **AND** system reattaches to running agent if exists
31: - **AND** system displays current file tree state
32: 
33: #### Scenario: Agent running on reconnect
34: - **WHEN** user reconnects and agent is still running
35: - **THEN** system establishes WebSocket to agent
36: - **AND** system resumes chat stream
37: 
38: ### Requirement: User can delete a session
39: The system SHALL allow users to remove sessions.
40: 
41: #### Scenario: Delete session with cleanup
42: - **WHEN** authenticated user deletes session "fix-auth-bug"
43: - **THEN** system terminates agent process if running
44: - **AND** system stops Fossil server
45: - **AND** system removes ~/.mimo/projects/my-app/sessions/fix-auth-bug/ directory
46: 
47: ### Requirement: Chat messages are stored persistently
48: The system SHALL store chat history in append-only JSONL format.
49: 
50: #### Scenario: Store user message
51: - **WHEN** user sends message "Add dark mode"
52: - **THEN** system appends to messages.jsonl: {type: "user", content: "Add dark mode", timestamp: "..."}
53: 
54: #### Scenario: Store agent message
55: - **WHEN** agent sends response "I'll help with that..."
56: - **THEN** system appends to messages.jsonl: {type: "agent", content: "...", timestamp: "..."}
57: 
58: #### Scenario: Stream agent response
59: - **WHEN** agent streams response in chunks
60: - **THEN** system buffers and stores complete message on finish
61: - **AND** system displays streaming updates via WebSocket
62: 
63: ### Requirement: Session shows file tree with change indicators
64: The system SHALL display project file tree with modification markers in the left buffer.
65: 
66: #### Scenario: Show modified files
67: - **WHEN** agent reports file changes
68: - **THEN** system marks files with [M] in file tree (left buffer)
69: - **AND** system updates changes buffer (right buffer)
70: 
71: #### Scenario: Show new files
72: - **WHEN** agent creates new files
73: - **THEN** system marks files with [?] in file tree (left buffer)
74: 
75: ### Requirement: Session shows file diffs with syntax highlighting
76: The system SHALL display file changes with language-appropriate syntax highlighting.
77: 
78: #### Scenario: View file diff
79: - **WHEN** user clicks on modified file
80: - **THEN** system displays diff with syntax highlighting
81: - **AND** system shows line additions and deletions
82: 
83: ### Requirement: User can navigate file tree
84: The system SHALL allow users to browse and open files.
85: 
86: #### Scenario: Find file
87: - **WHEN** user presses the configured keybinding for find_file
88: - **THEN** system prompts for file path
89: - **AND** system opens file in file buffer
90: 
91: #### Scenario: Click file in tree
92: - **WHEN** user clicks file in file tree
93: - **THEN** system loads file content into file buffer
94: 
95: ### Requirement: Keybindings are configurable
96: The system SHALL allow users to customize keybindings stored in ~/.mimo/config.yaml.
97: 
98: #### Scenario: Load custom keybindings
99: - **WHEN** user starts platform
100: - **THEN** system reads ~/.mimo/config.yaml
101: - **AND** system loads custom keybindings if defined
102: - **AND** system uses defaults for missing bindings
103: 
104: #### Scenario: Use default keybinding
105: - **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c (default cancel)
106: - **THEN** system executes cancel agent command
107: - **AND** keybinding is configurable via config.yaml
108: 
109: #### Scenario: Focus buffer with keybinding
110: - **WHEN** user presses C-x h
111: - **THEN** system focuses file buffer (left)
112: - **AND** system updates active buffer indicator
113: 
114: #### Scenario: Focus chat buffer
115: - **WHEN** user presses C-x j
116: - **THEN** system focuses chat buffer (center)
117: - **AND** system updates active buffer indicator
118: 
119: #### Scenario: Focus changes buffer
120: - **WHEN** user presses C-x l
121: - **THEN** system focuses changes buffer (right)
122: - **AND** system updates active buffer indicator

(End of file - total 122 lines)
</content>
```
## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 01:37:14)

Now I'm seeing the full picture. The spec says:

From session-management/spec.md:
- "Each session creates a worktree and starts a Fossil server"
- "system assigns auto-generated port (8000-9000 range)"
- "system starts Fossil server on assigned port"

From proposal.md:
- "Fossil server per session, mimo-agent process per session"

From design.md:
- "One Fossil server per session with auto-assigned ports"
- "mimo-agent (always clones from Fossil HTTP)"

So the intended architecture is:
1. Fossil server starts WHEN SESSION IS CREATED (not when agent connects)
2. Agent should connect to the Fossil server URL
3. Agent does a fossil clone from the HTTP server

But what I'm seeing in the code:
1. Fossil server starts when AGENT CONNECTS (index.tsx:211-240)
2. Agent doesn't receive the fossil server URL/base path needed for cloning
3. Agent already has a workDir passed at startup, not per-session

Let me check the tasks.md to see if there's more context about what was supposed to be done:

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-agent/README.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # mimo-agent
2: 
3: A TypeScript/Bun agent that connects to mimo-platform and provides file watching and ACP proxy functionality.
4: 
5: ## Installation
6: 
7: ```bash
8: cd packages/mimo-agent
9: bun install
10: ```
11: 
12: ## Building
13: 
14: Build the standalone binary:
15: 
16: ```bash
17: bun run build
18: ```
19: 
20: This creates `dist/mimo-agent` which is a self-contained executable.
21: 
22: ## Usage
23: 
24: ```bash
25: ./dist/mimo-agent --token <JWT_TOKEN> --platform <WEBSOCKET_URL> [--workdir <PATH>]
26: ```
27: 
28: ### Arguments
29: 
30: - `--token`: JWT token for authentication (required)
31: - `--platform`: WebSocket URL of the mimo-platform (required)
32: - `--workdir`: Working directory to watch for changes (optional, defaults to current directory)
33: 
34: ### Example
35: 
36: ```bash
37: ./dist/mimo-agent \
38:   --token eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9... \
39:   --platform ws://localhost:3000/ws/agent \
40:   --workdir /home/user/projects/my-app
41: ```
42: 
43: ## Features
44: 
45: ### File Watching
46: - Watches the working directory recursively for file changes
47: - Debounces changes (500ms) to batch rapid modifications
48: - Reports changes to the platform via WebSocket
49: - Ignores hidden files and common directories (node_modules, __pycache__)
50: 
51: ### ACP Proxy
52: - Receives ACP requests from the platform
53: - Spawns ACP agent process and proxies stdio
54: - Supports request cancellation (SIGTERM)
55: 
56: ### Reconnection
57: - Automatically reconnects on WebSocket disconnect
58: - Exponential backoff (max 5 attempts)
59: - Buffers changes during disconnect
60: 
61: ## Protocol
62: 
63: ### WebSocket Messages
64: 
65: **From Agent to Platform:**
66: 
67: ```json
68: {
69:   "type": "agent_ready",
70:   "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
71: }
72: ```
73: 
74: ```json
75: {
76:   "type": "file_changed",
77:   "files": [
78:     { "path": "src/app.js", "isNew": false },
79:     { "path": "src/new.ts", "isNew": true }
80:   ],
81:   "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
82: }
83: ```
84: 
85: ```json
86: {
87:   "type": "acp_response",
88:   "content": "Response content...",
89:   "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
90: }
91: ```
92: 
93: **From Platform to Agent:**
94: 
95: ```json
96: {
97:   "type": "ping"
98: }
99: ```
100: 
101: ```json
102: {
103:   "type": "acp_request",
104:   "command": "echo",
105:   "args": ["Hello, World!"]
106: }
107: ```
108: 
109: ```json
110: {
111:   "type": "cancel_request"
112: }
113: ```
114: 
115: ## Development
116: 
117: ```bash
118: # Run in development mode
119: bun run dev
120: 
121: # Run tests
122: bun test
123: ```
124: 
125: ## Architecture
126: 
127: ```
128: ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
129: │           mimo-platform             │
130: │  ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │
131: │  │   WebSocket Server            │  │
132: │  │   (/ws/agent)                 │  │
133: │  └────────────┬────────────────┘  │
134: └───────────────┼─────────────────────┘
135:                 │
136:                 │ WebSocket + JWT Auth
137:                 │
138: ┌───────────────┼─────────────────────┐
139: │               │                     │
140: │  ┌────────────▼────────────────┐   │
141: │  │       mimo-agent            │   │
142: │  │  ┌──────────────────────┐   │   │
143: │  │  │  File Watcher        │   │   │
144: │  │  │  (fs.watch)          │   │   │
145: │  │  └──────────────────────┘   │   │
146: │  │  ┌──────────────────────┐   │   │
147: │  │  │  ACP Process         │   │   │
148: │  │  │  (Bun.spawn)         │   │   │
149: │  │  └──────────────────────┘   │   │
150: │  └──────────────────────────────┘   │
151: │                                     │
152: └─────────────────────────────────────┘
153: ```

(End of file - total 153 lines)
</content>
````
## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 01:37:22)

Now I have a much clearer picture. Let me summarize what the OpenSpec artifacts say and compare with the implementation:

**From the spec (proposal.md line 36):**
> "Processes: Fossil server per session, mimo-agent process per session"

This says ONE mimo-agent per session! But the current implementation allows ONE agent to have multiple sessions (agent.sessionIds is an array).

**From design.md lines 42-44:**
> "Fossil as Intermediary Layer"
> "Provides consistent HTTP cloning interface for agents"
> "mimo-agent (always clones from Fossil HTTP)"

This says the agent should CLONE from the fossil server. But looking at task 11.5:
> "11.5 Implement Fossil clone from platform"

And the current implementation of mimo-agent - it receives `session_ready` but doesn't handle it, and it doesn't clone from fossil. It just watches a `workdir` that's passed in.

**Key gaps:**

1. **session-management/spec.md lines 9-12**: "system starts Fossil server on assigned port" - this should happen when session is CREATED, not when agent connects!

2. **The current implementation starts fossil server when agent connects** (index.tsx:211-239), but the spec says it should be started when the session is created.

3. **Agent needs to receive:**
   - The fossil server URL (not just port)
   - The checkout path to clone into
   - But currently agent doesn't handle this

4. **The proposal says "mimo-agent process per session"** but current design allows one agent to have multiple sessions.

Let me diagram this properly.

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                                         │

  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
  │  │ proposal.md says: "mimo-agent process per session"                               │  │
  │  │ implementation has: agent.sessionIds: string[] (multiple!)                       │  │
  │  │                                                                                   │  │
  │  │ IF ONE PER SESSION:                                                              │  │
  │  │   - Agent needs session info at spawn time                                       │  │
  │  │   - Agent needs fossil URL at spawn time                                         │  │
  │  │   - Agent needs checkout path at spawn time                                      │  │
  │  │   - Platform passes these when spawning agent                                     │  │
  │  │                                                                                   │  │
  │  │ IF ONE AGENT WITH MULTIPLE SESSIONS:                                             │  │
  │  │   - Agent must handle session_ready dynamically                                  │  │
  │  │   - Agent must manage multiple checkouts/watch directories                       │  │
  │  │   - Agent must switch context per session                                        │  │
  │  │   - Current impl doesn't support this                                            │  │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  │                                                                                         │
  │  Q2: WHEN DOES FOSSIL SERVER START?                                                   │
  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
  │  │ session-management/spec.md says: "AND system starts Fossil server"              │  │
  │  │                        "WHEN session is created"                                 │  │
  │  │                                                                                   │  │
  │  │ implementation starts it: WHEN AGENT CONNECTS                                    │  │
  │  │                                                                                   │  │
  │  │ IF SESSION CREATION:                                                             │  │
  │  │   - Server is ready BEFORE agent connects                                        │  │
  │  │   - Agent can immediately clone on connection                                    │  │
  │  │   - Session port stored in session.yaml                                          │  │
  │  │                                                                                   │  │
  │  │ IF AGENT CONNECTION:                                                             │  │
  │  │   - Server not available until agent shows up                                    │  │
  │  │   - Race condition: session created, agent not connected yet                     │  │
  │  │   - Can't use session without agent connected first                               │  │
  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
  │                                                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  MISSING DATA FLOW:
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                                                                                         │
  │  WHAT AGENT NEEDS TO KNOW:                                                            │
  │  - Fossil server base URL (e.g., "http://localhost:8080")                              │
  │  - Session's checkout path (where to clone/clone exists)                              │
  │  - Or: Full path to existing checkout (if cloned by platform)                         │
  │                                                                                         │
  │  WHAT SESSION CREATION DOES (per spec):                                               │
  │  - Clone repo → repo.fossil ✓ (implemented in sessions/routes.tsx:132-166)          │
  │  - Open checkout ✓ (implemented)                                                       │
  │  - Start fossil server ✗ (MISSING - should be here!)                                  │
  │                                                                                         │
  │  WHAT AGENT CURRENTLY RECEIVES:                                                       │
  │  - session_ready {sessionId, port} - but port alone is useless                       │
  │    without knowing:                                                                    │
  │    - The base URL of the platform (for HTTP connection)                               │
  │    - The session's checkout path                                                       │
  │                                                                                         │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

#### Summary

**The root causes:**

| Issue | spec.md | Implementation |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| **Fossil server start timing** | On session creation | On agent connection |
| **Agent per session** | "mimo-agent process per session" | One agent can have multiple sessions |
| **Agent receives session info** | Not specified, but implied | No handler for `session_ready` |
| **Platform base URL** | Agent clones from fossil HTTP | Agent doesn't know the URL |
| **Checkout path** | Agent should work in session checkout | Agent uses `--workdir` CLI arg |

**The fundamental design question you raised:**

> "agent should start before session_ready so the agent should have a opened port to connect"

This inverts the current assumption. The current code assumes:
1. Agent connects → 2. Platform looks up agent's sessions → 3. Start fossil servers → 4. Send `session_ready`

But you're suggesting:
1. Session created → 2. Start fossil server → 3. Agent connects → 4. Send `session_ready` with full info

This also means the agent needs to receive:
- Platform base URL (for fossil HTTP clones)
- Session checkout path (or handle `session_ready` to dynamically add watch directories)

Would you like me to:
1. Document these gaps formally in the OpenSpec artifacts?
2. Explore specific fixes to the architecture?
3. Look at what would need to change in session creation vs agent connection?

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 01:39:10)

The user is confirming my understanding is correct. Let me re-read:

1. Agent connects via WebSocket
2. Platform looks up agent's sessions
3. Platform starts fossil servers for each session
4. Platform sends `session_ready` {sessionId, port}
5. Agent should then:
   - Clone from fossil server using the port
   - Start ACP server (like opencode)

The missing pieces are:
1. Agent doesn't handle `session_ready` message
2. Agent doesn't know the platform base URL to construct the fossil clone URL
3. Agent doesn't have logic to clone from fossil
4. Agent needs to start an ACP server (not just spawn child processes, but actually host an ACP server that clients can connect to)

Let me look at what opencode does - or what ACP server looks like. The user mentioned "start the ACP server like opencode" - this suggests the agent should be running a local ACP server that the platform can communicate with, similar to how opencode works.

## Summary: What's Missing

|-----------|----------|--------|
| **`@agentclientprotocol/sdk`** | Installed & used for ACP server | Not installed in mimo-agent |
| **`session_ready` handler** | Handle in agent, clone fossil, start ACP | Falls through to "unknown message type" |
| **Session checkout path** | Agent needs to know where to work | Agent uses `--workdir` CLI arg, no dynamic assignment |
| **Fossil clone logic** | Agent clones from fossil server to checkout | Not implemented in agent |
| **ACP server startup** | Agent starts ACP server per session | Agent only proxies ACP requests, doesn't spawn ACP |

**Key design clarifications needed:**

1. **Does platform pre-create the checkout?** (Yes - in `sessions/routes.tsx:157-166`)

2. **How does agent know checkout path?**

   - Option B: Agent receives path in `session_ready`
   
3. **One agent per session or multi-session?**
   - proposal.md says "mimo-agent process per session"
   - Current agent has `sessionIds: string[]` (supports multiple)

4. **Does agent need to clone fossil at all?**
   - Checkout is already created by platform
   - Agent just needs the checkout path to watch files

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 01:42:27)

Now I have a clearer picture. Let me trace the complete flow:

1. Platform creates session with upstream/ and repo.fossil
2. Agent connects to platform via WebSocket
3. Platform looks up agent's sessions, starts fossil servers
4. Platform sends `session_ready` with sessionId, port, AND checkout path
5. Agent:
   - Receives `session_ready`
   - Clones from `http://localhost:<port>/` to checkout path
   - Spawns ACP process (like opencode)
   - Watches files in checkout
   - Proxies ACP messages between platform and ACP process

So the agent needs:
- `session_ready` handler
- Fossil clone logic
- Checkout path from message
- ACP server spawning (not just proxying)
- Multi-session management (multiple checkouts, multiple ACP processes)

# Skill: openspec-new-change

Start a new change using the experimental artifact-driven approach.

**Input**: The user's request should include a change name (kebab-case) OR a description of what they want to build.

**Steps**

1. **If no clear input provided, ask what they want to build**

   Use the **AskUserQuestion tool** (open-ended, no preset options) to ask:
   > "What change do you want to work on? Describe what you want to build or fix."

   From their description, derive a kebab-case name (e.g., "add user authentication" → `add-user-auth`).

   **IMPORTANT**: Do NOT proceed without understanding what the user wants to build.

2. **Determine the workflow schema**

   Use the default schema (omit `--schema`) unless the user explicitly requests a different workflow.

   **Use a different schema only if the user mentions:**
   - A specific schema name → use `--schema <name>`
   - "show workflows" or "what workflows" → run `openspec schemas --json` and let them choose

   **Otherwise**: Omit `--schema` to use the default.

3. **Create the change directory**
   ```bash
   openspec new change "<name>"
   ```
   Add `--schema <name>` only if the user requested a specific workflow.
   This creates a scaffolded change at `openspec/changes/<name>/` with the selected schema.

4. **Show the artifact status**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>"
   ```
   This shows which artifacts need to be created and which are ready (dependencies satisfied).

5. **Get instructions for the first artifact**
   The first artifact depends on the schema (e.g., `proposal` for spec-driven).
   Check the status output to find the first artifact with status "ready".
   ```bash
   openspec instructions <first-artifact-id> --change "<name>"
   ```
   This outputs the template and context for creating the first artifact.

6. **STOP and wait for user direction**

**Output**

After completing the steps, summarize:
- Change name and location
- Schema/workflow being used and its artifact sequence
- Current status (0/N artifacts complete)
- The template for the first artifact
- Prompt: "Ready to create the first artifact? Just describe what this change is about and I'll draft it, or ask me to continue."

**Guardrails**
- Do NOT create any artifacts yet - just show the instructions
- Do NOT advance beyond showing the first artifact template
- If the name is invalid (not kebab-case), ask for a valid name
- If a change with that name already exists, suggest continuing that change instead
- Pass --schema if using a non-default workflow

Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.

   - Clone from fossil proxy

   - Start ACP process

content: # Proposal: agent-fossil-session-handoff

## Why

The current implementation has a critical gap: when an agent connects, the platform sends `session_ready` with only `{sessionId, port}`, but the agent doesn't handle this message and lacks essential information to bootstrap sessions. The agent needs to receive checkout path (relative to its workdir), connect to fossil, clone/checkout, and spawn ACP processes. Currently, the checkout is created by the platform during session creation, but this is incorrect—the agent must create its own checkout from the fossil proxy. Additionally, `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` dependency is missing from mimo-agent despite being marked complete in tasks.

## What Changes

- **Platform sends enriched session_ready**: Include `checkoutPath` (relative to agent workdir), `platformUrl` for fossil clone, and full session context
- **Agent handles session_ready**: Parse message, clone from fossil proxy to checkout directory, spawn ACP process per session
- **Remove checkout creation from session bootstrap**: Platform creates repo.fossil only; agent creates checkout via fossil clone
- **Add ACP SDK dependency**: Install `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` in mimo-agent
- **Multi-session agent support**: Agent maintains map of sessions with their checkouts, ACP processes, and file watchers
- **Relative checkout paths**: Agent receives paths relative to its workdir, enabling portable operation

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `session-bootstrap`: Defines how agents receive session information and bootstrap their worktree by cloning from fossil proxy

### Modified Capabilities
- `agent-lifecycle`: Agent now handles `session_ready` message, maintains multi-session state, spawns ACP per session
- `session-management`: Session creation no longer creates checkout; only creates repo.fossil. Checkout creation moves to agent bootstrap phase.

## Impact

- **mimo-platform/src/index.tsx**: WebSocket handler sends enriched `session_ready` with checkout path and platform URL
- **mimo-platform/src/sessions/routes.tsx**: Remove `openFossilCheckout` call from session creation
- **mimo-platform/src/sessions/repository.ts**: Update path handling for relative paths
- **mimo-agent/package.json**: Add `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` dependency
- **mimo-agent/src/index.ts**: Add session management, `session_ready` handler, fossil clone logic, ACP process spawning per session
- **Message protocol**: `session_ready` message structure changes from `{sessionId, port}` to `{sessionId, port, checkoutPath, platformUrl}`

```

╰─────────╯

@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+# Proposal: agent-fossil-session-handoff
+
+## Why
+
+The current implementation has a critical gap: when an agent connects, the platform sends `session_ready` with only `{sessionId, port}`, but the agent doesn't handle this message and lacks essential information to bootstrap sessions. The agent needs to receive checkout path (relative to its workdir), connect to fossil, clone/checkout, and spawn ACP processes. Currently, the checkout is created by the platform during session creation, but this is incorrect—the agent must create its own checkout from the fossil proxy. Additionally, `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` dependency is missing from mimo-agent despite being marked complete in tasks.
+
+## What Changes
+
+- **Platform sends enriched session_ready**: Include `checkoutPath` (relative to agent workdir), `platformUrl` for fossil clone, and full session context
+- **Agent handles session_ready**: Parse message, clone from fossil proxy to checkout directory, spawn ACP process per session
+- **Remove checkout creation from session bootstrap**: Platform creates repo.fossil only; agent creates checkout via fossil clone
+- **Add ACP SDK dependency**: Install `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` in mimo-agent
+- **Multi-session agent support**: Agent maintains map of sessions with their checkouts, ACP processes, and file watchers
+- **Relative checkout paths**: Agent receives paths relative to its workdir, enabling portable operation
+
+## Capabilities
+
+### New Capabilities
+- `session-bootstrap`: Defines how agents receive session information and bootstrap their worktree by cloning from fossil proxy
+
+### Modified Capabilities
+- `agent-lifecycle`: Agent now handles `session_ready` message, maintains multi-session state, spawns ACP per session
+- `session-management`: Session creation no longer creates checkout; only creates repo.fossil. Checkout creation moves to agent bootstrap phase.
+
+## Impact
+
+- **mimo-platform/src/index.tsx**: WebSocket handler sends enriched `session_ready` with checkout path and platform URL
+- **mimo-platform/src/sessions/routes.tsx**: Remove `openFossilCheckout` call from session creation
+- **mimo-platform/src/sessions/repository.ts**: Update path handling for relative paths
+- **mimo-agent/package.json**: Add `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` dependency
+- **mimo-agent/src/index.ts**: Add session management, `session_ready` handler, fossil clone logic, ACP process spawning per session
+- **Message protocol**: `session_ready` message structure changes from `{sessionId, port}` to `{sessionId, port, checkoutPath, platformUrl}`
\ No newline at end of file
```

When to include design.md (create only if any apply):
- Cross-cutting change (multiple services/modules) or new architectural pattern
- New external dependency or significant data model changes
- Security, performance, or migration complexity
- Ambiguity that benefits from technical decisions before coding

Sections:

- **Goals / Non-Goals**: What this design achieves and explicitly excludes
- **Decisions**: Key technical choices with rationale (why X over Y?). Include alternatives considered for each decision.
- **Risks / Trade-offs**: Known limitations, things that could go wrong. Format: [Risk] → Mitigation
- **Migration Plan**: Steps to deploy, rollback strategy (if applicable)
- **Open Questions**: Outstanding decisions or unknowns to resolve

Focus on architecture and approach, not line-by-line implementation.
Reference the proposal for motivation and specs for requirements.

Good design docs explain the "why" behind technical decisions.

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-04 01:44:41)

Now I need to create the design artifact. This should explain the technical approach for implementing the change, including the architecture decisions, risks, and migration plan.

Let me think about the key design decisions:

1. **Session handoff protocol**: How does platform communicate session info to agent?
   - Decision: Single `session_ready` message with all sessions at once (simpler than incremental)
   - Alternative: Send one message per session

2. **Checkout path handling**: Relative vs absolute paths
   - Decision: Agent receives paths relative to its workdir, enabling portability
   - Platform computes relative path from session.checkoutPath to agent's configured workdir

3. **ACP process management**: One per session
   - Decision: Agent maintains Map<sessionId, SessionContext> with ACP process per session
   - Alternative: Single ACP process for all sessions (rejected - needs context switching)

4. **Fossil clone location**: 
   - Decision: Clone to checkoutPath relative to workdir
   - Alternative: Clone to temp dir then move (rejected - more complex)

5. **Error handling**: What if clone fails?
   - Decision: Agent sends error message back to platform, session marked as failed
   - Agent continues processing other sessions

Let me write the design document.

### Tool Call [completed]: openspec/changes/agent-fossil-session-handoff/design.md

**Timestamp:** 2026-04-04 01:44:55
**Parameters:**
filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/agent-fossil-session-handoff/design.md
content: # Design: agent-fossil-session-handoff

## Context

### Current State
- Platform creates checkout directory during session creation (`sessions/routes.tsx:157-166`)
- Fossil server starts when agent connects, not during session creation
- Agent sends `agent_ready` on connect but never handles `session_ready`
- Agent uses `--workdir` CLI arg for file watching, no per-session paths
- `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` is not installed despite task marked complete

### Problem
The agent receives `{sessionId, port}` but lacks:
1. Checkout path (relative to workdir)
2. Platform URL for fossil clone
3. Handler for `session_ready` message
4. Multi-session management

### Constraints
- Agent must support multiple concurrent sessions
- Checkout paths must be relative to agent workdir for portability
- Platform may be remote (not always localhost)
- One fossil server per session, one ACP process per session

## Goals / Non-Goals

**Goals:**
- Agent receives complete session info on connect
- Agent clones from fossil proxy to create checkout
- Agent spawns ACP process per session
- Agent maintains multi-session state
- Platform stops creating checkout during session bootstrap

**Non-Goals:**
- Real-time session addition/removal (future: agent would need to handle `session_added`/`session_removed` messages)
- Session migration between agents
- ACP process multiplexing (one ACP per session is the model)

## Decisions

### 1. Session Handoff Protocol

**Decision:** Single `session_ready` message with complete session array

**Rationale:**
- Simpler state management
- Agent can batch initialize all sessions
- Avoids race conditions with incremental messages
- Consistent with existing message patterns

**Message Structure:**
```json
{
  "type": "session_ready",
  "platformUrl": "http://localhost:3000",
  "sessions": [
    {
      "sessionId": "uuid-1",
      "port": 8080,
      "checkoutPath": "projects/abc/sessions/xyz/checkout"
    },
    {
      "sessionId": "uuid-2",
      "port": 8081,
      "checkoutPath": "projects/def/sessions/uvw/checkout"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Note:** `checkoutPath` is relative to agent's `--workdir`. Platform computes this using `path.relative(agent.workdir, session.checkoutPath)`.

**Alternative considered:** Individual `session_ready` per session
- Rejected: More complex state machine, potential ordering issues

### 2. Checkout Path Strategy

**Decision:** Relative paths computed by platform

**Rationale:**
- Agent knows its workdir, platform knows session paths
- Relative paths enable agent portability
- Platform already has both paths; computation is simple

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// Platform side (in index.tsx)
const relativeCheckoutPath = path.relative(agentWorkdir, session.checkoutPath);
```

**Alternative considered:** Absolute paths
- Rejected: Breaks if agent runs from different working directory

### 3. Multi-Session Architecture

**Decision:** Map<sessionId, SessionContext> with per-session ACP process

**Rationale:**
- Clean separation between sessions
- Each session has independent ACP lifecycle
- File watcher per session enables concurrent work

**Data Structure:**
```typescript
interface SessionContext {
  sessionId: string;
  checkoutPath: string;
  fossilUrl: string;      // http://localhost:<port>
  acpProcess: ChildProcess | null;
  fileWatcher: FSWatcher | null;
}

private sessions: Map<string, SessionContext> = new Map();
```

### 4. Bootstrap Sequence

**Decision:** Sequential clone → ACP spawn per session

**Sequence:**
```
session_ready received
│
├─ For each session:
│   ├─ Clone fossil → checkoutPath
│   │   fossil clone http://localhost:<port> <checkoutPath>
│   │
│   ├─ Spawn ACP in checkoutPath
│   │   ACP process runs in session context
│   │
│   ├─ Start file watcher on checkoutPath
│   │
│   └─ Store in sessions map
│
└─ Send agent_sessions_ready to platform
```

**Error handling:** If clone fails for one session, log error, continue others, report failures

### 5. ACP Integration

**Decision:** Use `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` for ACP communication

**Rationale:**
- Standard protocol implementation
- Handles message framing
- Already mentioned in tasks

**Alternative:** Custom stdio framing
- Rejected: Reinventing wheel, SDK handles edge cases

## Risks / Trade-offs

**[Risk] Clone fails after fossil server starts**
→ Agent sends error to platform, session marked as failed. User can retry.

**[Risk] Multiple agents for same session**
→ Platform assigns `assignedAgentId` to session. Only one agent per session.

**[Risk] Agent disconnect mid-clone**
→ Fossil server continues running (30s grace period). Agent reconnects and retries.

**[Risk] ACP process crash**
→ Agent detects via process exit, reports to platform, user can restart.

**[Trade-off] Sequential vs parallel bootstrap**
→ Sequential is simpler, parallel would be faster for many sessions.
→ Accepted: Most agents have 1-2 sessions, sequential is acceptable.

**[Risk] Relative path computation fails**
→ Platform must have agent's workdir stored. Agent sends workdir in initial handshake.

## Migration Plan

### Phase 1: Platform Changes
1. Update `session_ready` message in `index.tsx`
2. Remove `openFossilCheckout` from `sessions/routes.tsx`
3. Add `workdir` field to agent registration/handshake

### Phase 2: Agent Changes
1. Add `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` to package.json
2. Add `sessions` map and `SessionContext` interface
3. Implement `session_ready` handler
4. Add fossil clone logic
5. Add ACP spawn per session
6. Add file watcher per session
7. Update README with multi-session info

### Phase 3: Testing
1. Unit tests for relative path computation
2. Integration tests for agent bootstrap
3. E2E tests for multi-session scenarios
4. Test clone failures and recovery

### Rollback
- If issues arise, platform can revert to creating checkout (feature flag)
- Agent falls back to error reporting if `session_ready` missing required fields

## Open Questions

1. **Should agent send workdir in `agent_ready` message?**
   - Yes, platform needs this for relative path computation
   - Add `workdir` field to `agent_ready`

2. **What if agent's workdir doesn't include checkout path?**
   - This is a configuration error
   - Agent should report error and fail session

3. **Should platform validate workdir includes checkout?**
   - Optional validation, warn user on session assignment
   - Agent handles the error case

4. **ACP SDK vs opencode CLI?**
   - If opencode is an ACP-compatible CLI, agent spawns it directly
   - Need to confirm what ACP SDK provides vs spawning opencode directly

````

╰─────────╯

+# Design: agent-fossil-session-handoff
+
+## Context
+
+### Current State
+- Platform creates checkout directory during session creation (`sessions/routes.tsx:157-166`)
+- Fossil server starts when agent connects, not during session creation
+- Agent sends `agent_ready` on connect but never handles `session_ready`
+- Agent uses `--workdir` CLI arg for file watching, no per-session paths
+- `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` is not installed despite task marked complete
+
+### Problem
+The agent receives `{sessionId, port}` but lacks:
+1. Checkout path (relative to workdir)
+2. Platform URL for fossil clone
+3. Handler for `session_ready` message
+4. Multi-session management
+
+### Constraints
+- Agent must support multiple concurrent sessions
+- Checkout paths must be relative to agent workdir for portability
+- Platform may be remote (not always localhost)
+- One fossil server per session, one ACP process per session
+
+## Goals / Non-Goals
+
+**Goals:**
+- Agent receives complete session info on connect
+- Agent clones from fossil proxy to create checkout
+- Agent spawns ACP process per session
+- Agent maintains multi-session state
+- Platform stops creating checkout during session bootstrap
+
+**Non-Goals:**
+- Real-time session addition/removal (future: agent would need to handle `session_added`/`session_removed` messages)
+- Session migration between agents
+- ACP process multiplexing (one ACP per session is the model)
+
+## Decisions
+
+### 1. Session Handoff Protocol
+
+**Decision:** Single `session_ready` message with complete session array
+
+**Rationale:**
+- Simpler state management
+- Agent can batch initialize all sessions
+- Avoids race conditions with incremental messages
+- Consistent with existing message patterns
+
+**Message Structure:**
+```json
+{
+  "type": "session_ready",
+  "platformUrl": "http://localhost:3000",
+  "sessions": [
+    {
+      "sessionId": "uuid-1",
+      "port": 8080,
+      "checkoutPath": "projects/abc/sessions/xyz/checkout"
+    },
+    {
+      "sessionId": "uuid-2",
+      "port": 8081,
+      "checkoutPath": "projects/def/sessions/uvw/checkout"
+    }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+**Note:** `checkoutPath` is relative to agent's `--workdir`. Platform computes this using `path.relative(agent.workdir, session.checkoutPath)`.
+
+**Alternative considered:** Individual `session_ready` per session
+- Rejected: More complex state machine, potential ordering issues
+
+### 2. Checkout Path Strategy
+
+**Decision:** Relative paths computed by platform
+
+**Rationale:**
+- Agent knows its workdir, platform knows session paths
+- Relative paths enable agent portability
+- Platform already has both paths; computation is simple
+
+**Implementation:**
+```typescript
+// Platform side (in index.tsx)
+const relativeCheckoutPath = path.relative(agentWorkdir, session.checkoutPath);
+```
+
+**Alternative considered:** Absolute paths
+- Rejected: Breaks if agent runs from different working directory
+
+### 3. Multi-Session Architecture
+
+**Decision:** Map<sessionId, SessionContext> with per-session ACP process
+
+**Rationale:**
+- Clean separation between sessions
+- Each session has independent ACP lifecycle
+- File watcher per session enables concurrent work
+
+**Data Structure:**
+```typescript
+interface SessionContext {
+  sessionId: string;
+  checkoutPath: string;
+  fossilUrl: string;      // http://localhost:<port>
+  acpProcess: ChildProcess | null;
+  fileWatcher: FSWatcher | null;
+}
+
+private sessions: Map<string, SessionContext> = new Map();
+```
+
+### 4. Bootstrap Sequence
+
+**Decision:** Sequential clone → ACP spawn per session
+
+**Sequence:**
+```
+session_ready received
+│
+├─ For each session:
+│   ├─ Clone fossil → checkoutPath
+│   │   fossil clone http://localhost:<port> <checkoutPath>
+│   │
+│   ├─ Spawn ACP in checkoutPath
+│   │   ACP process runs in session context
+│   │
+│   ├─ Start file watcher on checkoutPath
+│   │
+│   └─ Store in sessions map
+│
+└─ Send agent_sessions_ready to platform
+```
+
+**Error handling:** If clone fails for one session, log error, continue others, report failures
+
+### 5. ACP Integration
+
+**Decision:** Use `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` for ACP communication
+
+**Rationale:**
+- Standard protocol implementation
+- Handles message framing
+- Already mentioned in tasks
+
+**Alternative:** Custom stdio framing
+- Rejected: Reinventing wheel, SDK handles edge cases
+
+## Risks / Trade-offs
+
+**[Risk] Clone fails after fossil server starts**
+→ Agent sends error to platform, session marked as failed. User can retry.
+
+**[Risk] Multiple agents for same session**
+→ Platform assigns `assignedAgentId` to session. Only one agent per session.
+
+**[Risk] Agent disconnect mid-clone**
+→ Fossil server continues running (30s grace period). Agent reconnects and retries.
+
+**[Risk] ACP process crash**
+→ Agent detects via process exit, reports to platform, user can restart.
+
+**[Trade-off] Sequential vs parallel bootstrap**
+→ Sequential is simpler, parallel would be faster for many sessions.
+→ Accepted: Most agents have 1-2 sessions, sequential is acceptable.
+
+**[Risk] Relative path computation fails**
+→ Platform must have agent's workdir stored. Agent sends workdir in initial handshake.
+
+## Migration Plan
+
+### Phase 1: Platform Changes
+1. Update `session_ready` message in `index.tsx`
+2. Remove `openFossilCheckout` from `sessions/routes.tsx`
+3. Add `workdir` field to agent registration/handshake
+
+### Phase 2: Agent Changes
+1. Add `@agentclientprotocol/sdk` to package.json
+2. Add `sessions` map and `SessionContext` interface
+3. Implement `session_ready` handler
+4. Add fossil clone logic
+5. Add ACP spawn per session
+6. Add file watcher per session
+7. Update README with multi-session info
+
+### Phase 3: Testing
+1. Unit tests for relative path computation
+2. Integration tests for agent bootstrap
+3. E2E tests for multi-session scenarios
+4. Test clone failures and recovery
+
+### Rollback
+- If issues arise, platform can revert to creating checkout (feature flag)
+- Agent falls back to error reporting if `session_ready` missing required fields
+
+## Open Questions
+
+1. **Should agent send workdir in `agent_ready` message?**
+   - Yes, platform needs this for relative path computation
+   - Add `workdir` field to `agent_ready`
+
+2. **What if agent's workdir doesn't include checkout path?**
+   - This is a configuration error
+   - Agent should report error and fail session
+
+3. **Should platform validate workdir includes checkout?**
+   - Optional validation, warn user on session assignment
+   - Agent handles the error case
+
+4. **ACP SDK vs opencode CLI?**
+   - If opencode is an ACP-compatible CLI, agent spawns it directly
+   - Need to confirm what ACP SDK provides vs spawning opencode directly

<instruction>
Create specification files that define WHAT the system should do.

Create one spec file per capability listed in the proposal's Capabilities section.
- New capabilities: use the exact kebab-case name from the proposal (specs/<capability>/spec.md).
- Modified capabilities: use the existing spec folder name from openspec/specs/<capability>/ when creating the delta spec at specs/<capability>/spec.md.

Delta operations (use ## headers):
- **ADDED Requirements**: New capabilities
- **MODIFIED Requirements**: Changed behavior - MUST include full updated content
- **REMOVED Requirements**: Deprecated features - MUST include **Reason** and **Migration**
- **RENAMED Requirements**: Name changes only - use FROM:/TO: format

Format requirements:
- Each requirement: `### Requirement: <name>` followed by description
- Use SHALL/MUST for normative requirements (avoid should/may)
- Each scenario: `#### Scenario: <name>` with WHEN/THEN format
- **CRITICAL**: Scenarios MUST use exactly 4 hashtags (`####`). Using 3 hashtags or bullets will fail silently.
- Every requirement MUST have at least one scenario.

MODIFIED requirements workflow:
1. Locate the existing requirement in openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md
2. Copy the ENTIRE requirement block (from `### Requirement:` through all scenarios)
3. Paste under `## MODIFIED Requirements` and edit to reflect new behavior
4. Ensure header text matches exactly (whitespace-insensitive)

Common pitfall: Using MODIFIED with partial content loses detail at archive time.
If adding new concerns without changing existing behavior, use ADDED instead.

Example:
```
## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: User can export data
The system SHALL allow users to export their data in CSV format.

#### Scenario: Successful export
- **WHEN** user clicks "Export" button
- **THEN** system downloads a CSV file with all user data

## REMOVED Requirements

### Requirement: Legacy export
**Reason**: Replaced by new export system
**Migration**: Use new export endpoint at /api/v2/export
```

Specs should be testable - each scenario is a potential test case.
</instruction>

<template>
<!-- Use this as the structure for your output file. Fill in the sections. -->
## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: <!-- requirement name -->
<!-- requirement text -->

#### Scenario: <!-- scenario name -->
- **WHEN** <!-- condition -->
- **THEN** <!-- expected outcome -->
</template>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: User can create an agent
4: The system SHALL allow users to create agents for sessions. Creating an agent generates a token and spawns mimo-agent process.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Create agent for session
7: - **WHEN** authenticated user creates agent for session "fix-auth-bug"
8: - **THEN** system generates JWT token {session_id: "...", exp: "..."}
9: - **AND** system spawns mimo-agent process with --token=<jwt> --platform=ws://localhost:4000/agent
10: - **AND** system stores agent.yaml with {token: "...", session_id: "...", status: "starting"}
11: - **AND** system displays "Agent starting..." in chat buffer
12: 
13: #### Scenario: Agent connects successfully
14: - **WHEN** mimo-agent connects via WebSocket with valid token
15: - **THEN** system updates agent.yaml status to "connected"
16: - **AND** system appends "Agent connected" to chat buffer
17: 
18: #### Scenario: Agent fails to start
19: - **WHEN** mimo-agent executable not found or crashes
20: - **THEN** system updates agent.yaml status to "failed"
21: - **AND** system displays error message in chat buffer
22: - **AND** system allows retry
23: 
24: ### Requirement: User can list active agents
25: The system SHALL display running agents for the current user.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: List active agents
28: - **WHEN** user navigates to agents page
29: - **THEN** system displays all agents with status: session name, status, pid, started_at
30: 
31: #### Scenario: Filter by status
32: - **WHEN** user filters agents by "connected" status
33: - **THEN** system displays only agents with status "connected"
34: 
35: ### Requirement: System can cancel current ACP request
36: The system SHALL allow users to cancel the current ACP agent request.
37: 
38: #### Scenario: Cancel current request with C-c C-c
39: - **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c in session
40: - **THEN** system sends cancel signal to current ACP request
41: - **AND** system appends "Request cancelled" to chat buffer
42: - **AND** agent remains connected and ready for next request
43: 
44: #### Scenario: Cancel when no active request
45: - **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c with no active ACP request
46: - **THEN** system displays "No active request to cancel"
47: 
48: ### Requirement: System can terminate agent
49: The system SHALL allow users to kill the entire agent process.
50: 
51: #### Scenario: Kill agent via command
52: - **WHEN** user executes kill-agent command
53: - **THEN** system terminates mimo-agent process
54: - **AND** system kills ACP child process
55: - **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "killed"
56: - **AND** system appends "Agent terminated" to chat buffer
57: 
58: #### Scenario: Agent process dies unexpectedly
59: - **WHEN** mimo-agent process exits unexpectedly
60: - **THEN** system detects via WebSocket disconnect
61: - **AND** system kills any orphaned ACP process
62: - **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "died"
63: 
64: ### Requirement: Agent persists across disconnects
65: The system SHALL maintain agent process when user disconnects.
66: 
67: #### Scenario: Agent continues after disconnect
68: - **WHEN** user disconnects from session
69: - **THEN** mimo-agent process continues running
70: - **AND** agent continues working on assigned tasks
71: 
72: #### Scenario: Agent reports changes after reconnect
73: - **WHEN** user reconnects after agent made file changes
74: - **THEN** system receives buffered change notifications
75: - **AND** system updates file tree with changes
76: 
77: ### Requirement: Agent uses token for authentication
78: The system SHALL validate JWT tokens from agents.
79: 
80: #### Scenario: Valid token authentication
81: - **WHEN** agent connects with valid JWT token
82: - **THEN** system identifies session from token claims
83: - **AND** system authorizes WebSocket connection
84: 
85: #### Scenario: Invalid token rejection
86: - **WHEN** agent connects with invalid or expired token
87: - **THEN** system rejects WebSocket connection
88: - **AND** system logs authentication failure

(End of file - total 88 lines)
</content>
